findings.message: Adding "email" to frontend_rum_allowed_query_params captures the user's email address from the URL query string into RUM telemetry. The allowlist's stated purpose is marketing attribution (UTM params); email is PII that is not needed for that purpose. It flows through routes/frontend_rum.py:64 into frontend_metrics.record_page_view(), persisting PII in telemetry/metrics. This is unnecessary PII collection into telemetry.
findings.suggestion: Remove the "email" entry. Keep the allowlist limited to the non-PII UTM attribution params (utm_source/medium/campaign/term/content). If per-user analytics are genuinely required, use a non-identifying pseudonymous ID rather than collecting raw email into telemetry.
